Fixed in frontend/src/ipc.ts: one TextDecoder per connection with {stream:true}. Verified end-to-end against a live PTY — 10x U+FFFD before, 0 after.

Not a font bug. The '?' glyphs were U+FFFD from UTF-8 sequences split across WebSocket frames (see the ipc.ts decode fix), not missing Nerd Font/powerline coverage. Glyph probe across all three renderers came back clean.
Fixed in frontend/src/tabs.ts: dedupe unchanged grid sizes + 80ms trailing debounce before notifying the PTY.

RESOLVED against ghostty-web. API surface: xterm.js exposes parser.registerOscHandler/registerCsiHandler/registerDcsHandler/registerEscHandler; ghostty-web exposes NO OSC handler registration at all — only OSC 8 hyperlinks, and its own types note the URI is 'not yet exposed in simplified API'. Verified empirically on xterm.js, not from docs: registered handlers for OSC 7 and OSC 133 and wrote real sequences — OSC 7 returned 'file://wemosd1/Users/shady/Documents/repos/nocx', OSC 133 returned all five markers A, B, C, D;0, D;127 including exit codes. So AD-5/AD-6 hold on xterm.js with no backend sniffing; on ghostty-web they would require patching the library.
Bake-off harness delivered its purpose — three renderers side by side, each with its own live PTY, drove the renderer decision. Tabs stay in the codebase for re-testing future candidates.

Fixed in internal/pty/pty_local.go: withUTF8Locale() defaults LANG=en_US.UTF-8 when the inherited environment states no locale at all (LC_ALL/LC_CTYPE/LANG all absent), which is the Finder/Dock launch case. An inherited locale is never overridden, including a deliberate LANG=C. Table-driven test covers all five cases; go test -race, gofumpt and golangci-lint all green.

Quality gates wired: .githooks/pre-commit (4 gates, verified red->green by the worker and re-verified independently by the coordinator), make hooks + make ci, .github/workflows/ci.yml (macos-latest backend, ubuntu frontend, golangci-lint pinned v1.64.8). First CI run still unverified — tracked separately.

ghostty-web dropped from the project (user decision 2026-07-21). These three bugs are ghostty-web-specific rendering defects (imageSmoothing/DPR blur, Retina cell seams from fractional canvas-2d rects, scroll reset on new output) and no longer describe any code we ship. xterm.js is the VT frontend per ADR-0001.

Superseded: the whole bead was 'verify ghostty-web exposes OSC 7/133, patch it if absent'. ghostty-web is dropped, and the spike nocx-dej already proved xterm.js exposes parser.registerOscHandler with OSC 7 and all five OSC 133 markers captured end-to-end (ADR-0001). Nothing left to verify or patch. The actual handler wiring lives in nocx-5mn.2 (OSC 7 cwd events) and nocx-5mn.3.

Done and verified by the coordinator: zero ghostty-web library references outside docs/decisions/, all grep survivors are competitor positioning; ADR-0001 untouched; TerminalRenderer + wterm intact; pre-commit hook green (gofumpt, golangci-lint, go test -race, tsc); npm run build succeeds. Not committed — conservative profile.
All three children complete: scaffold, composition root + slog, quality gates (hook + Makefile + GitHub Actions). First CI run still unverified — tracked in nocx-3xf.

Run 29822238195 (workflow_dispatch on main) green: backend 2m30s — gofumpt, golangci-lint v1.64.8, go build, go test -race all pass; frontend npm ci + tsc + vite build pass. Root cause of the first failure was golangci-lint-action@v4 shipping a Go-1.24-built binary against go.mod's go 1.26; fixed in 0adab78 by installing the linter with go install under setup-go's Go 1.26.

Control plane + session registry landed and reviewed over four rounds. JSON-RPC 2.0 open/close/resize + exit notification over text frames; binary data plane framed as version|msgType|16-byte session-id|payload with the metadata type reserved; server-authoritative ids from crypto/rand; PTY created at open's size (proven end-to-end via stty size); real session.Reg behind an injected PTYFactory (AD-8), concrete wiring at the composition root only. Spine updated: frontend is a peer quality surface, CI claims corrected to release-only. 64 tests passing, all gates green.

Frontend gated exactly like Go: eslint+prettier+vitest wired into pre-commit, make ci and CI; 39 frontend tests; connect() regression mutation-verified; golden wire vector pinned on both sides.

One WebSocket, N sessions: WSClient is multi-session with a per-session streaming TextDecoder; tabs share one client. Backend already multiplexed — proven by TestWSServer_TwoSessionsOneConnection_Isolation rather than rewritten.

Reconnect end to end: backend replay ring keyed by byte-offset with attach/ack, sessions survive a dropped WS; frontend tracks per-session offsets, throttled acks, reconnects with backoff and reattaches, handling resumed vs reset (full renderer reset + fresh decoder).

AD-10 credit flow control: per-session 64KB in-flight window derived from ring state, FairChunk yield so a flooding tab cannot monopolise the shared writer. Fixed a stall bug where the window waited for a fully-acked stream — a condition the client can never meet.

All five children complete: one WS with binary data plane + JSON-RPC control plane, server-authoritative session ids, multi-session multiplexing, AD-9 replay ring with reconnect, AD-10 per-session credit and fair scheduling.
